sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with right shoulder pain that began approximately three weeks ago after a fall while painting a house.
The pain is described as intermittent, but always present, and is exacerbated by lifting overhead.
The patient has taken ibuprofen and acetaminophen (Tylenol) for pain relief, with a reported pain level of 4 out of 10 when taking medication.
Without medication, the pain level is 6 out of 10.
The patient reports numbness and tingling in the arm initially after the injury, but this symptom has resolved.
Past medical history: The patient has no history of previous shoulder pain or injuries.
Review of systems: The patient denies any other symptoms.
Current medications: Ibuprofen as needed for pain; Acetaminophen (Tylenol) as needed for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has limited active and passive range of motion in the right shoulder, with tenderness to the greater tuberosity of the humerus.
The patient has good strong bilateral hand grips, brisk capillary refill of less than three seconds, and equal and bilateral response to sensation and touch.
Test Results: The right shoulder x-ray reveals no fracture or bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are most likely due to rotator cuff tendinopathy.
Problem list: Rotator cuff tendinopathy; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed ibuprofen as needed for pain.
I ordered an MRI of the right shoulder to rule out any other conditions.
I referred the patient to physical therapy for 6-8 weeks.
I recommend the patient take Tylenol for pain relief if they can tolerate it.
I will see the patient in two weeks to evaluate their progress and make additional treatment plans as needed.
Follow-up:
I will see the patient in two weeks.
